intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or entirely subterranean. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but shows signs of severe neglect or dormancy.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only dark, undisturbed soil. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains dark, damp soil with no visible vegetative growth.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains the white rabbit anchor and a single, struggling green sprout/stem. The plant is in a state of extreme physiological stress.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: A metallic spoon has been introduced into the P1/P2 area (T-2 to Current). A white cylindrical object (likely a lotion or chemical bottle) has been introduced near the P1 pot in the current frame.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T3: Initial state shows minimal growth; P3 anchor is visible.
temporal_deltas.T3_to_T2: Introduction of a metallic spoon into the yellow pot (P1) area.
temporal_deltas.T2_to_T1: Lighting shift; significant loss of detail, suggesting a potential camera obstruction or extreme low-light condition.
temporal_deltas.T1_to_current: Introduction of a white cylindrical container adjacent to the yellow pot.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of visible foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests either total plant death or deep dormancy. P3 is the only survivor, showing a singular, weak stem. The environment appears to be treated as a storage surface rather than a functional botanical habitat.
anomalies: Metallic spoon in P1/P2 pot area
White cylindrical bottle introduced in current frame
Persistent lack of vegetative growth across 5-day sequence
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a system in decline. Over the 5-day observation period, there has been no growth or recovery. Instead, the pots have become receptacles for non-botanical items (spoon, bottle). The Pothos (P3) is the only specimen showing any biological presence, though it is severely compromised. The absence of the other registered plants indicates a systemic loss of the original collection.
